tool_name: Plate Bending Force Calculator — Sheet Metal Forming / Press Brake Force and Capacity Utilization Check
category: Sheet Metal / Press Brake / Plate Forming
standards: DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending
ISO 16092-3 — Machine tools safety — Press brakes
ISO 12100 — Safety of machinery — General principles for design — Risk assessment and risk reduction
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production
VDI 3441 — Statistical testing of the operational and positional accuracy of machine tools
Manufacturer press brake manual — Capacity chart and tooling specifications

inputs.name: Bending Process Type
inputs.hint: Select the press brake bending process. Air bending: workpiece contacts V-die at three points. Bottoming: punch forces material into die at full stroke. Coining: punch penetrates material surface for minimal springback

inputs.name: Material Family
inputs.hint: Material family classification. Selection affects recommended V-die ratio, springback characteristics, and minimum bend radius

inputs.name: Ultimate Tensile Strength (UTS)
inputs.hint: Ultimate tensile strength of the workpiece material. Low-carbon steel: 270-400 MPa. Stainless 304: 500-700 MPa. Aluminum 6061: 260-310 MPa. Use material certificate or standard reference
inputs.uncertainty_source: Material certificate or standard reference data uncertainty

inputs.name: Yield Strength (YS)
inputs.hint: Yield strength of workpiece material. Low-carbon steel: 180-250 MPa. Stainless 304: 200-350 MPa. Aluminum 6061: 140-240 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: Material certificate or standard reference data uncertainty

inputs.name: Elastic Modulus (Young's Modulus)
inputs.hint: Elastic modulus of workpiece material. Steel: 200 GPa. Stainless: 193 GPa. Aluminum: 69 GPa. Copper: 110 GPa. Titanium: 110 GPa

inputs.name: Workpiece Thickness
inputs.hint: Nominal thickness of the workpiece being bent. Sheet metal: 0.5-6 mm. Plate: 6-100 mm

inputs.name: Bend Length (Part Width)
inputs.hint: Length of the bend line (width of the workpiece perpendicular to the bend axis). Longer bends require higher total force

inputs.name: V-Die Opening Width
inputs.hint: Width of the V-die opening. Recommended V-die = 8× to 12× material thickness for air bending

inputs.name: Punch Tip Radius
inputs.hint: Radius at the tip of the punch. Typically 0.5-2.0 mm for sheet metal. Affects inside bend radius and force distribution

inputs.name: Target Bend Angle
inputs.hint: Desired included bend angle. 90° is a standard right-angle bend. Acute bends < 90° may require special tooling
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle gauge or protractor measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Bending Force Coefficient (K1)
inputs.hint: Empirical bending force coefficient. Standard air bending: 1.42. Bottoming: 5. Coining: 10+. Increase for tight radii, high-strength materials

inputs.name: Process Force Multiplier
inputs.hint: Force multiplier for the selected bending process. Air bending: 1.0. Bottoming: 2.0-5.0. Coining: 5.0-10.0. Determined by process type and depth of penetration

inputs.name: Grain Direction Factor
inputs.hint: Factor accounting for material grain orientation. Across grain: 1.0 (default). With grain: 1.1-1.3 (higher force). Diagonal: 0.9-1.0. Bending with the grain increases cracking risk

inputs.name: Friction and Lubrication Factor
inputs.hint: Empirical factor for lubrication conditions. Well lubricated: 0.9-1.0. Dry: 1.0-1.15. Poor lubrication: 1.15-1.3. Affects sliding friction between workpiece and tooling

inputs.name: Safety Factor for Force Calculation
inputs.hint: Safety factor applied to the required force. ISO 12100 risk assessment: minimum 1.2. Increase for unknown material properties or worn tooling

inputs.name: Press Brake Nominal Capacity
inputs.hint: Rated maximum force of the press brake. Common capacities: 500 kN (50 ton), 1000 kN (100 ton), 2000 kN (200 ton). Check machine nameplate

inputs.name: Usable Bed Length (Between Side Frames)
inputs.hint: Maximum usable bed length between press brake side frames. Common: 2000-4000 mm. Longer beds reduce force per meter capacity

inputs.name: Upper Tool (Punch) Capacity
inputs.hint: Maximum allowable force per meter on the upper tool (punch). Varies by punch type, material, and heat treatment. Check tooling manufacturer specification

inputs.name: Lower Die (V-Die) Capacity
inputs.hint: Maximum allowable force per meter on the lower die (V-die). Check die manufacturer capacity chart

inputs.name: Minimum Permitted Inside Radius
inputs.hint: Minimum acceptable inside bend radius. Used to check if the predicted radius meets print requirement. Set to 0 if no minimum requirement

inputs.name: Off-Center Load Factor
inputs.hint: Factor for off-center loading. Centered load (symmetric): 1.0. Partially off-center: 1.25-1.5. Full off-center (one side frame): 1.5-2.0. Off-center reduces effective machine capacity

inputs.name: Operator Guarding Verified (ISO 16092-3)
inputs.hint: Operator guarding and safety devices verified per ISO 16092-3. Includes light curtains, two-hand controls, and rear guarding

inputs.name: Tooling Verification Completed
inputs.hint: Punch and die tooling verified for correct mounting, alignment, and capacity rating. Confirm in machine setup checklist

inputs.name: Thickness Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of thickness measurement. Caliper/micrometer resolution: typically ±0.03 mm. Laser gauge: ±0.01 mm

inputs.name: Material Strength Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Relative standard uncertainty of tensile strength and yield strength values. Material certificate: ±1-3%. Generic reference: ±5-10%

inputs.name: Force Coefficient Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Relative standard uncertainty of the empirical bending force coefficient. DIN standard coefficients: ±5%. Custom estimates: ±10-20%

formulas.name: V-Die Opening to Thickness Ratio
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Recommended V-die opening to thickness ratio for air bending: 8:1 to 12:1
edge_cases: plate_thickness_mm <= 0: Cannot compute ratio — invalid thickness
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Base Air Bending Force (kN)
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Base air bending force formula: F = K1 × UTS × t² × L / V
assumptions: Uniform material properties across bend length
edge_cases: v_die_opening_mm <= 0: Cannot compute force — invalid V-die opening
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Process-Adjusted Force (kN)
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Process-adjusted force: air bending × multiplier for bottoming or coining
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Required Press Force with Adjustment Factors
formulas.expression: required_press_force_kN = process_adjusted_force_kN * material_direction_factor * friction_lubrication_factor * safety_factor_force * off_center_load_factor
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Final required force including material, friction, safety, and loading adjustment factors
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Required Tonnage (Metric Tons)
formulas.reference: ISO 80000 — Force conversion: 1 kN = 0.10197 metric ton-force (using standard gravity 9.80665 m/s²)
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Force Per Meter of Bend Length
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Specific force per unit bend length for tooling capacity comparison
edge_cases: bend_length_mm <= 0: Cannot compute force per meter
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Predicted Inside Radius (Air Bending)
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Air bending inside radius ≈ V/6.67. Bottoming inside radius ≈ V/12. Coining: punch radius + 0.1 mm
assumptions: Sharp punch tip assumed for air bending
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Springback Index
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Springback tendency index. Higher values indicate greater springback. Typical threshold: >0.01 = significant springback expected
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Machine Utilization Ratio
formulas.reference: VDI 3441 — Machine capacity utilization: required force / rated capacity. Target: <0.8 for safe operation
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Upper Tool Utilization Ratio
formulas.reference: VDI 3441 — Upper tool utilization: force per meter / punch capacity per meter. Target: <0.9 for safe operation
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Lower Die Utilization Ratio
formulas.reference: VDI 3441 — Lower die utilization: force per meter / die capacity per meter. Target: <0.9 for safe operation
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Combined Utilization Coefficient (UC)
formulas.reference: VDI 3441 — Combined utilization coefficient: the highest utilization among all three capacity constraints
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Combined Utilization Uncertainty (u_c_UC)
formulas.reference: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM — Combined standard uncertainty for UC using RSS method with sensitivity coefficients = 1
edge_cases: plate_thickness_mm <= 0: Cannot compute thickness relative uncertainty
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Capacity Margin
formulas.reference: VDI 3441 — Capacity margin: positive value indicates machine has reserve capacity for this operation
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Status Code Determination
formulas.reference: VDI 3441 — Force capacity limit status classification
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

tensile_strength_positive.error_msg: Ultimate tensile strength must be positive.

machine_capacity_positive.error_msg: Machine nominal capacity must be positive.

usable_bed_length_positive.error_msg: Usable bed length must be positive.

upper_tool_capacity_positive.error_msg: Upper tool capacity per meter must be positive.

lower_die_capacity_positive.error_msg: Lower die capacity per meter must be positive.

bend_angle_valid.error_msg: Target bend angle must be between 1° and 180°.

v_die_ratio_minimum.error_msg: V-die opening to thickness ratio is below the safe minimum of 4:1. Risk of die damage and poor bend quality.

operator_guarding_required.error_msg: Operator guarding must be verified per ISO 16092-3 before proceeding.

tooling_verification_required.error_msg: Tooling verification must be completed before proceeding.

smart_warnings.message: PRESS BRAKE OVERLOAD: The required bending force exceeds the machine rated capacity. Reduce bend length, use a larger V-die opening, select a different process (air bending instead of bottoming/coining), or move to a larger capacity machine.
PRESS BRAKE NEAR CAPACITY LIMIT: The required bending force approaches the machine rated capacity. Proceed with caution. Consider increasing V-die opening or reducing bend length for a safety margin.
UPPER TOOL OVERLOAD: Force per meter exceeds the upper tool (punch) capacity. Risk of tool damage or failure. Use a stronger punch or distribute the load over a longer tool section.
LOWER DIE OVERLOAD: Force per meter exceeds the lower die (V-die) capacity. Risk of die deformation or cracking. Use a stronger die or larger V-die opening.
V-DIE TO THICKNESS RATIO BELOW RECOMMENDED RANGE: For air bending, V/die ratio should be 6:1 to 12:1. Below 6:1 increases force requirement and risk of die damage.
V-DIE TO THICKNESS RATIO ABOVE RECOMMENDED RANGE: For air bending, ratio >12:1 may result in poor dimensional accuracy and excessive springback.

INSIDE RADIUS BELOW MINIMUM REQUIREMENT: The predicted inside bend radius is smaller than the minimum permitted radius. Increase V-die opening or use a different punch radius to meet print requirements.
SIGNIFICANT SPRINGBACK EXPECTED: Springback index exceeds 0.01. Large springback is expected. Consider overbending, coining, or reducing the bend radius. CNC compensation may be required.
PROCESS FORCE MULTIPLIER REVIEW REQUIRED: Air bending typically requires a process multiplier of 1.0. Values >3 suggest bottoming or coining. Verify process selection and multiplier are correct.
MATERIAL TENSILE-TO-YIELD RATIO VERY LOW: The UTS/YS ratio is below 1.05, indicating limited strain-hardening capacity. Increased risk of cracking during bending. Verify material properties.
OPERATOR GUARDING NOT VERIFIED: Operator safety devices must be verified per ISO 16092-3 before starting bending operations.
TOOLING NOT VERIFIED: Punch and die tooling must be verified for correct mounting and capacity rating per ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1.
SIGNIFICANT OFF-CENTER LOAD: Off-center load factor exceeds 1.3. Off-center bending reduces effective machine capacity and increases ram deflection risk. Verify load distribution.

smart_warnings.condition: UC >= 0 && UC <= 0.8 && operator_guarding_verified == true && tooling_verified == true
smart_warnings.message: PRESS BRAKE CAPACITY ADEQUATE: All capacity checks within safe limits (UC ≤ 0.8). Guarding and tooling verified. Safe to proceed.

fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation
Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Required force exceeds machine capacity. Risk of mechanical failure, ram damage, or operator injury.
fmea.control_measure: VDI 3441 — Capacity check with utilization ratio

fmea.effect: Force per meter exceeds punch capacity. Risk of punch fracture, tip chipping, or bending.
fmea.control_measure: Tooling manufacturer specification — force per meter check

fmea.effect: Force per meter exceeds die capacity. Risk of die cracking, V-opening deformation, or die insert failure.
fmea.control_measure: Die manufacturer specification — force per meter check

fmea.effect: Bend radius smaller than print requirement. Part rejection.
V-die opening to thickness ratio outside safe range. Increased force, poor bend quality, or die damage.
Large springback causes incorrect final bend angle. Requires rework or overbend compensation.
fmea.control_measure: DIN 8586 — Overbend compensation or coining process

fmea.effect: Safety devices not verified. Risk of operator injury from pinch points or flying debris.
Tooling condition unknown. Risk of incorrect mounting, wear damage, or capacity mismatch.

error_propagation.method: GUM ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 — LPU first-order Taylor expansion

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on zero/negative thickness, zero bend length, zero V-die opening, zero/negative material strengths, zero machine/tooling capacities. Block on missing operator guarding or tooling verification.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: standard air bending 90° in low-carbon steel, bottoming process, coining, high-strength steel edge case, machine overload scenario, tooling overload, combined UC > 1.0, uncertainty computation, springback classification.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST expression engine.
Run formulas F1-F15 in deterministic order.
Force formula: F = K1 × UTS × t² × L / V × multiplier × material_factor × friction_factor × safety × off_center_factor.
All factor adjustments default to 1.0 except safety factor (1.2).
V-die ratio must be >= 4:1 minimum for any process.
Inside radius prediction differs by process: air bending ≈ V/6.67, bottoming ≈ V/12, coining ≈ punch radius + 0.1 mm.
Springback index = YS × R_i / (E × t). Threshold: >0.01 = significant springback.
Utilization = required / capacity for machine, upper tool, and lower die separately.
Combined UC = highest utilization among all three constraints.
Capacity margin = machine capacity - required force (positive = safe).
Status code based on UC thresholds: >1.0 overload, >0.9 near limit, >0.8 adequate monitor, <=0.8 adequate.
Uncertainty from thickness, material strength, and force coefficient combined via RSS.
Safety gates: operator guarding and tooling verification must be true to proceed.
All utilization values are dimensionless ratios.
Use null for unavailable values, not Infinity.

metadata.description: Press brake plate bending force and capacity utilization calculator per DIN 8586, VDI 3441, and ISO 16092-3. Computes required bending force, machine capacity utilization, upper and lower tooling utilization, predicted inside radius, springback index, and combined utilization coefficient (UC) for air bending, bottoming, and coining processes on sheet metal and plate.
tuv_checklist: ISO 12100 risk assessment for force overload considered
ISO 7438 minimum bend radius check verified
ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM uncertainty propagation implemented
Material property data quality assessment outlined
Tooling capacity verification per manufacturer specification
metadata.calibration_trace: Force calculations based on DIN 8586 empirical coefficients. Machine capacity references traceable to manufacturer nameplate. Uncertainty values per ISO 80000 and ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM. Thickness uncertainty: ±0.03 mm, material strength uncertainty: ±3%, force coefficient uncertainty: ±5%.
